reviewNote: Editorial mappings pending sign-off by a qualified compliance SME. EQUIVALENT edges in particular must be reviewed before customers rely on cross-framework propagation.

edges.rationale: NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) information system security policy is implemented for digital-infrastructure entities by CIR 2024/2690 Annex 3.1 security policy requirement.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) security roles and responsibilities map one-to-one to CIR Annex 3.2 roles and responsibilities for network and information system security.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) risk assessment policy corresponds to CIR Annex 4.2 risk assessment methodology and criteria.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident response procedures are specified by CIR Annex 5.1 incident handling policy and procedures.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident detection overlaps CIR Annex 5.2 monitoring and logging, but CIR adds explicit logging duties.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) business continuity planning corresponds to CIR Annex 6.1 business continuity and disaster recovery plans.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) backup management corresponds to CIR Annex 6.2 backup management and data restoration.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier risk assessment corresponds to CIR Annex 7.1 supply chain security policy.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) contractual security requirements correspond to CIR Annex 7.2 supplier security requirements in contracts.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) vulnerability handling corresponds to CIR Annex 8.1 vulnerability handling and disclosure.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) security awareness training corresponds to CIR Annex 9.1 awareness and training programmes.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(h) cryptographic policy corresponds to CIR Annex 10.1 use of cryptography and encryption.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) access control policy corresponds to CIR Annex 11.1 access control policies and procedures.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(j) multi-factor authentication deployment corresponds to CIR Annex 11.2 multi-factor authentication.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) asset management and inventory corresponds to CIR Annex 12.1 asset management and inventory.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment policy corresponds to CIR Annex 13.1 security effectiveness assessment and testing.
NIS2 Art.20(1) management approval overlaps DORA Art.5 management body accountability, but DORA is ICT-risk specific with broader duties.
NIS2 Art.20(2) management cybersecurity training corresponds to DORA Art.5(4) management ICT risk training.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) risk assessment policy overlaps DORA Art.6/8 ICT risk assessment methodology; DORA is ICT-scoped and more prescriptive.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) information system security policy overlaps DORA Art.9(4) ICT security policies, procedures, protocols and tools.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident detection overlaps DORA Art.10 anomalous activity detection; DORA adds alert-threshold specifics.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident classification corresponds to DORA Art.18 ICT-related incident classification.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident response procedures overlap DORA Art.17 ICT incident management process; DORA adds financial-sector workflow duties.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) lessons learned corresponds to DORA Art.13 post-incident review and lessons learned.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) business continuity plan overlaps DORA Art.11 ICT business continuity policy; DORA is ICT-scoped.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) crisis management corresponds to DORA Art.11(7) crisis management process.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) backup management corresponds to DORA Art.12 backup policy.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) disaster recovery planning overlaps DORA Art.11(3) ICT response and recovery plans.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) BC/DR testing overlaps DORA Art.11(6) disaster recovery testing; DORA prescribes frequency for critical functions.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) BC/DR testing covers backup restoration testing (DORA Art.12) plus wider exercise scope.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) business impact analysis corresponds to DORA Art.11(2) BIA for ICT disruption.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier risk assessment overlaps DORA Art.28(4) pre-contract due diligence; DORA is contract-lifecycle specific.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) contractual security requirements overlap DORA Art.30 minimum contractual provisions; DORA mandates specific clauses.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier monitoring corresponds to DORA Art.28 ongoing provider risk monitoring.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) secure development lifecycle corresponds to DORA Art.9 secure acquisition, development and maintenance.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) patch management corresponds to DORA Art.9 vulnerability and patch management.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) vulnerability assessments correspond to DORA Art.25 vulnerability assessments.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) penetration testing corresponds to DORA Art.25 penetration testing.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(h) cryptographic policy corresponds to DORA Art.9(2) cryptography and key management.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) privileged access management corresponds to DORA Art.9(4)(c) privileged access management.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) identity management and access reviews correspond to DORA Art.9(4)(c) identity and access management.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) asset management overlaps DORA Art.8(1) ICT asset inventory; DORA is ICT-asset scoped.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(j) MFA deployment corresponds to DORA Art.9(4)(d) strong authentication for critical systems.
NIS2 Art.23(1) significant incident notification overlaps DORA Art.17/19 detection-to-notification workflow; regimes and recipients differ.
NIS2 Art.23(4)(a) 24h early warning parallels DORA Art.19 major incident reporting workflow; deadlines and templates differ.
NIS2 Art.23(4)(b) 72h notification parallels DORA reporting deadline tracking; different deadline scheme (initial/intermediate/final).
NIS2 Art.23(4)(d) final report parallels DORA standard incident reporting templates; content requirements differ.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) information system security policy corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.1 policies for information security.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) security roles and responsibilities correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.2.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident classification corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.25 assessment and decision on information security events.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident response procedures correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.26 response to information security incidents.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) lessons learned corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.27 learning from information security incidents.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) business continuity overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.29 information security during disruption; ISO is security-continuity scoped.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) backup management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.13 information backup.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier risk assessment corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.19 information security in supplier relationships.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) contractual security requirements correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.20 addressing security within supplier agreements.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier monitoring corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.22 monitoring, review and change management of supplier services.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) secure development lifecycle corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.25 secure development life cycle.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) vulnerability handling overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities; NIS2 adds disclosure duties.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) patch management is a subset of ISO 27001 A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) security awareness training corresponds to ISO 27001 A.6.3 awareness, education and training.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(h) cryptographic policy corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.24 use of cryptography.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) personnel background checks correspond to ISO 27001 A.6.1 screening.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) access control policy corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.15 access control.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) privileged access management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.2 privileged access rights.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) identity management and access reviews correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.18 access rights.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) asset management and inventory corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.9 inventory of information and other associated assets.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(j) MFA deployment overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.5 secure authentication; ISO covers wider authentication controls.
NIS2 Art.23(4)(a) regulatory early warning is related to ISO 27001 A.6.8 internal event reporting but targets authorities, not internal channels.
DORA Art.9(2) cryptography and key management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.24 use of cryptography.
DORA Art.9(4)(c) identity and access management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.16 identity management.
DORA Art.9(4)(c) privileged access management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.2 privileged access rights.
DORA Art.9 vulnerability and patch management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities.
DORA Art.12 backup policy corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.13 information backup.
DORA Art.17 ICT incident management process corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.24 incident management planning and preparation.
DORA Art.13 post-incident review corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.27 learning from information security incidents.
DORA evidence preservation and forensic readiness corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.28 collection of evidence.
DORA Art.8(1) ICT asset inventory overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.9; ISO also covers information assets beyond ICT.
DORA Art.30 minimum contractual provisions overlap ISO 27001 A.5.20 supplier agreements; DORA mandates sector-specific clauses.
DORA security logging and SIEM overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.15 logging; DORA adds detection use-case duties.
DORA network security and segmentation overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.22 segregation of networks.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) risk assessment policy overlaps NIST CSF ID.RA-05 risk understanding and prioritization.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident detection overlaps NIST CSF DE.CM-01 network monitoring.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) incident response procedures overlap NIST CSF RS.MA-01 incident response plan execution.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) backup management corresponds to NIST CSF PR.DS-11 backups created, protected, maintained, and tested.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) supplier risk assessment overlaps NIST CSF GV.SC-06 supplier due diligence.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) awareness training corresponds to NIST CSF PR.AT-01 personnel awareness and training.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(h) encryption deployment overlaps NIST CSF PR.DS-01 data-at-rest protection.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) access reviews overlap NIST CSF PR.AA-05 access permissions and least privilege.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(j) MFA deployment overlaps NIST CSF PR.AA-03 users, services, and hardware are authenticated.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) asset management covers hardware inventories (NIST CSF ID.AM-01) plus software and information assets.
NIS2 Art.23(4)(b) 72h notification overlaps NIST CSF RS.CO-02 stakeholder notification; NIS2 targets a regulator with a fixed deadline.
DORA Art.5 management body accountability corresponds to NIST CSF GV.RR-01 leadership accountability for cybersecurity risk.
DORA ICT risk appetite and tolerance corresponds to NIST CSF GV.RM-02 risk appetite and tolerance statements.
DORA threat intelligence monitoring corresponds to NIST CSF ID.RA-02 cyber threat intelligence.
DORA Art.18 incident classification corresponds to NIST CSF RS.MA-03 incidents categorized and prioritized.
DORA disaster recovery testing overlaps NIST CSF ID.IM-02 improvements from tests and exercises.
DORA ICT risk register is related to NIST CSF ID.RA-05 risk prioritization as its record-keeping substrate.
DORA ongoing provider risk monitoring corresponds to NIST CSF GV.SC-07 supplier risks understood, assessed and monitored.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(a) risk treatment plan overlaps DORA risk treatment and residual risk acceptance; DORA adds explicit residual-risk sign-off.
NIS2 residual risk acceptance overlaps DORA risk treatment and residual risk acceptance; DORA frames it within the ICT risk framework.
NIS2 policy review cycle corresponds to DORA policy governance and periodic review.
NIS2 risk treatment plan corresponds to NIST CSF ID.RA-06 risk responses chosen, prioritized, planned, tracked and communicated.
NIS2 residual risk acceptance is one element of NIST CSF ID.RA-06 risk response management.
NIS2 policy review cycle corresponds to NIST CSF GV.PO-02 policy reviewed, updated, communicated and enforced.
NIS2 all-hazards approach overlaps NIST CSF ID.RA-03 identification of internal and external threats; NIS2 extends to physical and non-cyber hazards.
NIS2 management body oversight of cybersecurity measures overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.4 management responsibilities.
NIS2 management body accountability for non-compliance overlaps NIST CSF GV.RR-01 leadership accountability for cybersecurity risk.
NIS2 staff cybersecurity training access overlaps ISO 27001 A.6.3 awareness, education and training.
NIS2 security metrics and KPIs overlap NIST CSF GV.OV-03 evaluation of cybersecurity risk management performance.
NIS2 incident escalation procedures correspond to NIST CSF RS.MA-04 incidents escalated or elevated as needed.
NIS2 incident escalation procedures overlap DORA alert triage and escalation workflow; DORA focuses on detection-stage alerts.
NIS2 incident communication procedures overlap NIST CSF RS.CO-03 information sharing with designated stakeholders.
NIS2 incident response team establishment is part of ISO 27001 A.5.24 incident management planning and preparation.
NIS2 severity and impact assessment in notification overlaps NIST CSF RS.AN-08 incident magnitude estimation and validation.
NIS2 threat type and root cause in the final report overlaps NIST CSF RS.AN-03 root cause analysis.
NIS2 indicators of compromise in notification relate to NIST CSF RS.AN-07 collection and preservation of incident data.
NIS2 informing recipients and users about significant threats overlaps NIST CSF RS.CO-02 stakeholder notification of incidents.
NIS2 informing service recipients about significant threats overlaps DORA client and user communication for incidents.
NIS2 public disclosure of significant incidents relates to NIST CSF RC.CO-04 public updates on incident recovery.
NIS2 ICT product and service security assessment corresponds to NIST CSF ID.RA-09 authenticity and integrity assessed prior to acquisition and use.
NIS2 ICT product and service security assessment overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.21 ICT supply chain security management.
NIS2 supply chain incident notification overlaps NIST CSF GV.SC-08 inclusion of suppliers in incident planning, response and recovery.
NIS2 supply chain incident notification overlaps DORA provider incident notification obligations; DORA is contract-driven.
NIS2 vulnerability disclosure policy corresponds to NIST CSF ID.RA-08 processes for receiving, analyzing and responding to vulnerability disclosures.
NIS2 security testing in acquisition and development corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.29 security testing in development and acceptance.
NIS2 security testing in acquisition and development overlaps NIST CSF PR.PS-06 secure software development practices.
NIS2 network segmentation and architecture security corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.22 segregation of networks.
NIS2 network segmentation overlaps NIST CSF PR.IR-01 protection of networks from unauthorized logical access.
NIS2 network segmentation and architecture security corresponds to DORA network segmentation controls.
NIS2 change management for network and information systems corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.32 change management.
NIS2 change management for network and information systems corresponds to DORA secure change management.
NIS2 internal security audits correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.35 independent review of information security.
NIS2 internal security audits overlap the DORA ICT internal audit programme; DORA prescribes audit-plan governance.
NIS2 basic cyber hygiene practices overlap NIST CSF PR.AT-01 general cybersecurity awareness and training.
NIS2 role-specific cybersecurity training corresponds to NIST CSF PR.AT-02 specialized role training.
NIS2 role-specific training is a targeted subset of ISO 27001 A.6.3 awareness, education and training.
NIS2 phishing and social engineering awareness is a specific topic within ISO 27001 A.6.3 awareness, education and training.
NIS2 cryptographic key management is covered by ISO 27001 A.8.24 use of cryptography including key management.
NIS2 human resources security policy corresponds to NIST CSF GV.RR-04 cybersecurity included in human resources practices.
NIS2 human resources security policy overlaps ISO 27001 A.6.2 terms and conditions of employment.
NIS2 physical access control corresponds to ISO 27001 A.7.2 physical entry controls.
NIS2 physical access control corresponds to NIST CSF PR.AA-06 physical access managed, monitored and enforced.
NIS2 physical access control overlaps DORA physical and environmental security; DORA also covers environmental threats.
NIS2 continuous authentication solutions overlap NIST CSF PR.AA-03 authentication of users, services and hardware.
NIS2 secured voice, video and text communications overlap ISO 27001 A.5.14 information transfer security.
NIS2 secured communications overlap NIST CSF PR.DS-02 protection of data-in-transit.
NIS2 cybersecurity information sharing arrangements overlap ISO 27001 A.5.6 contact with special interest groups.
NIS2 information sharing arrangements overlap NIST CSF ID.RA-02 receipt of cyber threat intelligence from sharing forums.
NIS2 information sharing arrangements overlap the DORA information-sharing arrangement policy; DORA is financial-sector specific.
NIS2 voluntary notification of cyber threats relates to NIST CSF ID.RA-02 threat intelligence sharing.
DORA internal ICT governance and control framework overlaps NIST CSF GV.PO-01 cybersecurity risk policy established and enforced.
DORA ICT risk roles, responsibilities and segregation corresponds to NIST CSF GV.RR-02 roles and authorities established and enforced.
DORA segregation of ICT risk duties overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.3 segregation of duties.
DORA digital operational resilience strategy overlaps NIST CSF GV.RM-01 risk management objectives established.
DORA policy governance and periodic review corresponds to NIST CSF GV.PO-02 policy reviewed, updated and enforced.
DORA ICT internal audit programme corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.35 independent review of information security.
DORA critical audit finding remediation overlaps NIST CSF ID.IM-01 improvements identified from evaluations.
DORA resourcing for digital resilience corresponds to NIST CSF GV.RR-03 adequate resources allocated commensurate with risk strategy.
DORA management reporting of ICT risk overlaps NIST CSF GV.OV-03 performance evaluation and review.
DORA comprehensive ICT risk management framework overlaps NIST CSF GV.RM-01 risk management objectives; DORA prescribes framework content.
DORA threat and vulnerability monitoring overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.7 threat intelligence.
DORA threat and vulnerability monitoring overlaps NIST CSF ID.RA-01 vulnerabilities identified, validated and recorded.
DORA risk treatment and residual risk acceptance corresponds to NIST CSF ID.RA-06 risk responses chosen, planned and tracked.
DORA major change ICT risk assessment overlaps NIST CSF ID.RA-07 changes and exceptions managed and assessed for risk impact.
DORA risk reassessment after incidents and testing overlaps NIST CSF ID.IM-03 improvements from operational processes.
DORA ICT RMF review and improvement overlaps NIST CSF GV.OV-02 risk management strategy review and adjustment.
DORA information asset and data inventory corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.9 inventory of information and associated assets.
DORA information asset and data inventory overlaps NIST CSF ID.AM-07 inventories of data and metadata.
DORA dependency mapping overlaps NIST CSF GV.OC-05 understanding of outcomes and services the organization depends on.
DORA third-party ICT dependency identification corresponds to NIST CSF ID.AM-04 inventories of supplier-provided services.
DORA asset classification scheme corresponds to ISO 27001 A.5.12 classification of information.
DORA asset classification overlaps NIST CSF ID.AM-05 asset prioritization by classification and criticality.
DORA network and system architecture documentation overlaps NIST CSF ID.AM-03 network communication and data flow representations.
DORA business process and data flow mapping corresponds to NIST CSF ID.AM-03 authorized network communication and data flow representations.
DORA secure configuration baselines correspond to ISO 27001 A.8.9 configuration management.
DORA secure configuration baselines correspond to NIST CSF PR.PS-01 configuration management practices.
DORA access recertification overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.18 access rights review.
DORA access recertification overlaps NIST CSF PR.AA-05 access permissions managed and reviewed.
DORA data security controls overlap NIST CSF PR.DS-01 protection of data-at-rest.
DORA data security controls overlap NIST CSF PR.DS-02 protection of data-in-transit.
DORA endpoint and server protection corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.1 user endpoint devices security.
DORA malware protection corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.7 protection against malware.
DORA malware protection overlaps NIST CSF PR.PS-05 prevention of unauthorized software installation and execution.
DORA log protection and traceability corresponds to NIST CSF PR.PS-04 log records generated and available for monitoring.
DORA log protection and traceability overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.15 logging.
DORA physical and environmental security overlaps ISO 27001 A.7.1 physical security perimeters.
DORA physical and environmental security overlaps NIST CSF PR.IR-02 protection from environmental threats.
DORA secure change management corresponds to ISO 27001 A.8.32 change management.
DORA ICT capacity and performance controls correspond to ISO 27001 A.8.6 capacity management.
DORA ICT capacity and performance controls correspond to NIST CSF PR.IR-04 adequate resource capacity for availability.
DORA data integrity controls overlap NIST CSF PR.DS-01 protection of integrity of data-at-rest.
DORA remote and third-party access control overlaps ISO 27001 A.6.7 remote working security.
DORA availability and performance monitoring overlaps NIST CSF DE.CM-09 monitoring of hardware, software and runtime environments.
DORA network performance and security monitoring corresponds to NIST CSF DE.CM-01 network monitoring for adverse events.
DORA network monitoring overlaps ISO 27001 A.8.16 monitoring activities.
DORA alert thresholds and incident triggers overlap NIST CSF DE.AE-08 incident declaration on defined criteria.
DORA alert triage and escalation workflow overlaps NIST CSF RS.MA-02 incident report triage and validation.
DORA incident roles and playbooks overlap ISO 27001 A.5.24 incident management planning and preparation.
DORA incident roles and playbooks overlap NIST CSF ID.IM-04 incident response plans established and maintained.
DORA client and user communication for incidents overlaps NIST CSF RS.CO-02 stakeholder notification of incidents.
DORA separated and resilient backup arrangements overlap ISO 27001 A.8.13 information backup; DORA adds segregation requirements.
DORA separated and resilient backup arrangements overlap NIST CSF PR.DS-11 backups created, protected, maintained and tested.
DORA recovery time and point objectives overlap ISO 27001 A.5.30 ICT readiness for business continuity.
DORA crisis and incident communication plan overlaps NIST CSF RC.CO-03 communication of recovery progress to stakeholders.
DORA alternative operating arrangements overlap ISO 27001 A.8.14 redundancy of information processing facilities.
DORA alternative operating arrangements overlap NIST CSF PR.IR-03 resilience mechanisms for normal and adverse situations.
DORA plan updates after tests and incidents overlap NIST CSF ID.IM-02 improvements identified from tests and exercises.
DORA digital operational resilience testing programme overlaps NIST CSF ID.IM-02 improvements from security tests and exercises.
DORA scenario-based and tabletop exercises overlap NIST CSF ID.IM-02 improvements identified from exercises.
DORA testing findings remediation overlaps NIST CSF ID.IM-01 improvements identified from evaluations.
DORA provider information security standards overlap ISO 27001 A.5.20 security within supplier agreements.
DORA provider information security standards overlap NIST CSF GV.SC-05 supply chain security requirements in contracts.
DORA risk assessment before contracting ICT services for critical functions corresponds to NIST CSF GV.SC-06 pre-contract due diligence.
DORA access, audit and inspection rights are contract clauses within ISO 27001 A.5.20 supplier agreements.
DORA service levels and provider monitoring correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.22 monitoring and review of supplier services.
DORA provider monitoring overlaps NIST CSF DE.CM-06 monitoring of external service provider activities.
DORA provider incident notification obligations overlap NIST CSF GV.SC-08 supplier inclusion in incident planning and response.
DORA subcontracting chain identification overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.21 ICT supply chain security management.
DORA provider assurance and audits overlap NIST CSF GV.SC-07 ongoing supplier risk assessment and monitoring.
DORA cloud and hosting resilience controls correspond to ISO 27001 A.5.23 information security for cloud services.
DORA information-sharing arrangement policy overlaps ISO 27001 A.5.6 contact with special interest groups.
DORA threat intelligence use from sharing arrangements corresponds to NIST CSF ID.RA-02 threat intelligence received from sharing forums.
